title: acer caesium subsp. giraldii
intro: Acer caesium subsp. giraldii, also known as the Gansu maple, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the family Sapindaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the bark, leaves, and roots of the Gansu maple are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues.
